title: Start reading
subtitle: Pick how you want to read, then point me at the story.
step.0.title: Style
step.1.title: Story
step.2.title: Options
mode.dehydrate.label: Rewrite & polish
mode.dehydrate.description: Trim the filler, tighten the prose, and make it read smoother — without losing what makes it fun.
mode.interactive.label: Interactive reading
mode.interactive.description: Read along, and at key moments you call the shots on what happens next.
novel.file.label: Upload a text file
novel.internet.label: Find it online
novel.internet.title.label: Story title
novel.internet.author.label: Author (optional)
novel.internet.source.label: Source
novel.internet.source.placeholder: Paste a link, or just describe where I can read it
novel.internet.source.tooltip: Tell me where I can read it — a link, a search hint, anything. It has to be a page I can open and read in the browser, not a PDF or TXT file I'd have to download.
novel.internet.source.hint: Has to be a readable web page — no PDFs or TXT downloads.
options.dehydrate.basic.grammarFix.label: Fix typos, grammar & punctuation
options.dehydrate.basic.grammarFix.toolt: Like a picky copyeditor: fix typos, grammar, and clunky phrasing, and clean up the punctuation (matching quotes, consistent marks) so it reads smooth.
options.dehydrate.basic.webSlangFilter.t: Swap out the overused web-fiction clichés and tired memes — the 'he gasped, pupils constricting', 'a cold glint flashed in his eyes', 'without batting an eye' kind of lines — so it stops feeling generic.
options.dehydrate.basic.preachRemoval.to: Cut the parts where the author butts in to lecture or dump lore — especially worldbuilding disguised as the character's inner monologue — and turn it into something you experience instead.
options.dehydrate.depth.dehydrate.label: Trim & speed up
options.interactive.frequency.label: Interaction frequency
